skill.name: gws-sheets
skill.description: Use the Google Workspace CLI to inspect, read, create, and update Google Sheets resources.
skill.summary: Manage Google Sheets through documented Google Workspace CLI commands.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found these are Markdown code spans, documentation links, and expected Google Workspace CLI examples. The real risk is capability based: the skill guides an agent to run external gws commands that can read or change Google Sheets data after user authorization.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Spreadsheet Read and Write Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use gws sheets commands that can create, read, and batch update Google Sheets. This is expected behavior, but it can modify user data after authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented command surface includes create, get, batchUpdate, and schema-driven command construction. The risk is from authorized data access and mutation, not from hidden malicious code.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The backtick, path traversal, hardcoded URL, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are caused by Markdown examples, relative documentation links, and Google API documentation references. No prompt injection or malicious instruction was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible documentation, not executable Ruby, shell, or cryptographic code. The referenced URLs point to Google developer documentation, and the relative paths point to adjacent skill documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation Required
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires the gws command-line tool and tells agents to inspect and run Google Sheets commands. Users should review spreadsheet IDs, parameters, and write operations before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly documents gws command usage. This is a legitimate skill mechanism, but command execution can affect live Google Sheets data.

content.user_title: Manage Google Sheets from AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Teams need reliable spreadsheet updates without manually building every Google Sheets API call. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through gws commands for Sheets operations.
actual_capabilities: Shows the base gws sheets command pattern for resources and methods.
Points users to append and read helper skills for common row workflows.
Lists core spreadsheet resources, including spreadsheets, sheets, values, and developer metadata.
Documents create, get, getByDataFilter, and batchUpdate methods at a high level.
Instructs agents to inspect method schemas before building parameters and flags.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and Google Workspace authentication to be configured.
Does not include full parameter examples for every Sheets API method.
Can only access spreadsheets allowed by the authenticated Google account.
Write operations can change live spreadsheet data and should be reviewed first.

use_cases.title: Operations Reporting
use_cases.description: Update status trackers, read active ranges, and prepare spreadsheet changes from an AI assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Data Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Inspect available Sheets methods and read spreadsheet data before planning cleanup or validation tasks.

use_cases.title: Workspace Automation
use_cases.description: Build repeatable Google Workspace CLI commands for creating spreadsheets and applying structured updates.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Sheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google Sheets skill to inspect the read helper and show the safest command plan for reading a spreadsheet range.

prompt_templates.title: Append a Row
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Sheets append helper to prepare a command that adds one reviewed row to my spreadsheet.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the schema inspection flow for a Sheets method and explain which parameters I must provide before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Batch Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Google Sheets batchUpdate workflow, list the requests to review, and wait for approval before running write commands.

output_examples.input: I need to read rows from a spreadsheet tab.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the read helper, asks for the spreadsheet ID and range, then presents a command plan for review.

output_examples.input: Create a spreadsheet for weekly metrics.
output_examples.output: The assistant inspects the create method, lists required fields, and prepares a gws sheets create command for approval.

output_examples.input: Apply several formatting and value updates.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends batchUpdate, separates each requested change, and asks for confirmation before modifying the spreadsheet.

best_practices: Inspect the method schema before building parameters or JSON flags.
Confirm spreadsheet IDs, ranges, and write requests before running commands.
Use the read and append helper skills for common row-level tasks.
anti_patterns: Do not run write commands against production spreadsheets without user approval.
Do not paste sensitive spreadsheet data into prompts unless it is required.
Do not assume default ranges or sheet names when the user has not provided them.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant use the Google Workspace CLI for Google Sheets resources and methods.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The gws CLI must be installed and authorized for the Google account that owns or can access the spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can it change spreadsheet data?
faq.answer: Yes. Methods such as batchUpdate and append can change data, so write commands should be approved before execution.

faq.question: Can it read private spreadsheets?
faq.answer: It can read spreadsheets only when the authenticated account has permission to access them.

faq.question: Does it provide every Sheets API parameter?
faq.answer: No. It tells the assistant to inspect gws schema output before constructing method parameters.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it uses external commands and can access authorized spreadsheet data.
